---
title: "model_diagnostics_simple"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-28"
---
#plotting the simple diagnostics
#not doing expansive param recovery etc. due to time constraints (just the bare minimum)
# (also param recovery would probably be hampered by the model & data logic differing on the group mean trustworthiness value? -- fixed mean vs adaptive mean. can't really bother to fix that right now, could be an issue technically?)

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse, # Data manipulation and plotting
  dplyr,
  here, # Robust file paths
  ggplot2, #ggplot
  cmdstanr,
  loo,
  bayesplot #Plotting MCMC
)
#paths of the fits
results <- readRDS(here("models/fits/results_paths.rds"))
```

```{r}
#3. CHECKING DIAGNOSTICS (WBA)
# slim saves are plain lists now, so we access fields with $ instead of calling methods
check_diagnostics <- function(fit_slim, label) {
  diag_smry <- fit_slim$diagnostic_summary        # already computed at save time, no method call needed
  min_ebfmi <- suppressWarnings(min(diag_smry$ebfmi, na.rm = TRUE))
  smry <- fit_slim$summary |> filter(!is.na(rhat))
  n_fail <- smry |> filter(rhat > 1.01 | ess_bulk < 400 | ess_tail < 400) |> nrow()
  cat(sprintf("%-25s | Divs: %3d | Min E-BFMI: %.2f | Failures: %d\n",
              label,
              sum(diag_smry$num_divergent),
              min_ebfmi,
              n_fail))
}

cat("\n=== WBA Diagnostics ===\n")
invisible(
  imap(results, function(r, name) {
    wba_slim <- readRDS(r$wba_file)
    check_diagnostics(wba_slim, paste0("WBA_", name))
  })
)

#4. LOO COMPARISON PER DATASET (hopefully correct)
loo_results <- imap(results, function(r, dataset_name) {
  
  wba_slim <- readRDS(r$wba_file)
  sba_slim <- readRDS(r$sba_file)

  # extract loo objects
  wba_loo <- wba_slim$loo
  sba_loo <- sba_slim$loo

  # --- SAFETY CHECK: Pareto-k diagnostics ---
  check_k <- function(loo_obj, name) {
    k <- loo_obj$diagnostics$pareto_k
    cat("\n", name, "Pareto-k summary:\n")
    print(summary(k))
    
    if (any(k > 0.7, na.rm = TRUE)) {
      warning(name, ": high Pareto-k detected → LOO may be unreliable")
    }
  }

  cat("\n==============================\n")
  cat("DATASET:", dataset_name, "\n")

  check_k(wba_loo, "WBA")
  check_k(sba_loo, "SBA")

  # LOO comparison (safe)
  comparison <- loo_compare(list(WBA = wba_loo,
                                SBA = sba_loo))

  cat("\n=== LOO Comparison ===\n")
  print(comparison)

  # --- WEIGHTS (ONLY if safe) ---
  w <- NULL

  if (all(wba_loo$diagnostics$pareto_k < 0.7, na.rm = TRUE) &&
      all(sba_loo$diagnostics$pareto_k < 0.7, na.rm = TRUE)) {

    w <- loo_model_weights(list(WBA = wba_loo,
                                SBA = sba_loo),
                           method = "stacking")

    cat("\n=== Model Weights (stacking) ===\n")
    print(w$weights)

  } else {
    cat("\n⚠ Skipping weights: unstable PSIS (high Pareto-k)\n")
  }

  list(
    comparison = comparison,
    weights = w
  )
})

#struggling with getting the weights unfortunately (for SBA) as its pareto k blows the fuck up
#maybe a mistake somewhere in the process? SBA is fully deterministic (fixed=TRUE), or in the model specification?
#we can assume itd weigh = 1 WBA for all of these... because SBA is misspecified somehow
```
#model recovery
